summary: A stellar 1984 soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix, highlighted by a roaring Casey Jones opener and standout Althea, Mighty Swell and China > Rider performances.
review: These three reviews rave about the pristine 84 soundboard tape, calling it “amazing” and “excellent,” while also celebrating the show’s high energy – especially the explosive Casey Jones opening, the soaring Althea and Mighty Swell in set one, and a snappy China > I Know You Rider start to the second set. The only dent is a reviewer’s lukewarm feeling toward Day Job.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviewers give high marks (5, 5, 4) and focus on both superb recording quality and strong musical moments; only a single minor criticism appears.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the 84 boards as "amazing" with crisp, clear mix; audience recording also praised but secondary to the superb soundboard source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set remembered for a blistering Casey Jones opener and memorable Althea/Mighty Swell moments; second set kicks off with an energetic China > Rider segue, followed by tight transitions and enthusiastic vocal leads.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s vocal lead is highlighted (“Bobby leads us into the song”), adding excitement to the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s keyboard tone in ’84 is called distinct and trippy, a standout element of the performance.
